text: I've been this size since I was zero days old
Two Argyles — the angel stuffed inside the demon who was already named Argyle. Lazy and incredible.
Argyle, the self-litigating lawyer who can't read a room — considers suicide at a handshake, everything he likes is tyrant-coded, yet takes his cases pro bono on principle.
Argyle's a fickle little man — a fan of whoever did the last cool thing, no loyalty.
The jacket's 'morally binding' — Benny's verdict: 'oof, worse. It's merch.'
We're hot rod people now / build me a hood out of a box of scraps in a cave.
Bram's bad-bet credibility: defending the totaled car as a 'tactical loss.'
'I love and fear that man' — Josh the self-aware GM, who didn't group his sixty-three tokens because he's a smart man.
Bram's bet: Johnny takes Aut's deal in the end — the oath has a price they haven't found yet.
Pip can't let it go: Johnny's god ghosted him and he STILL won't ask the all-knowing radio god the one real question.
Aut thinks Anzu's arm is fine as it is — the most useless successful perception check ever.
The inverted-dice curse — a seven knows everything, a thirty-four knows nothing.
Milk and whiskey: the magic combo that cracks open backstories.
'Just us' instead of justice — the party's selfishness dressed up as an ideal.
Chuck the butler — offered any name in the world, they picked the most boring one possible.
It's always Chuck — whatever they drag out of the void inevitably gets named Chuck.
Don't eat the gingerbread house, it's a war hero — Johnny's gonna eat it anyway.
A bowl of children's guns as a treat / hand the baby a revolver, it's tradition.
Radio people are always evil — and everyone fancy turns out to be a radio person eventually. Write it down.
Benny and the Jets
Benny parries a Ki-Rin's hoof with a fucking butterfly (NOT a butterfly sword — the sword ruins it).
Jorge's recaps are always a disaster — 'immediately wrong.'
There's no barber. (Mike swears the one-haircut flat-top barber is real.)
